hero.question: US x Iran meeting by April 10, 2026?
hero.prob_take: At 54%, this market is screaming "we have no idea" rather than pricing in real diplomatic momentum, which means the crowd is overvaluing the binary event risk. The 20-point dump in 24h suggests smart money is betting against the narrative, and they're probably right: direct US-Iran meetings require political cover neither side needs before next year's US election cycle heats up.

daily_take.headline: Iran Talks Just Tanked 20 Points. Smart Money Sees a Bluff.
daily_take.deck: US-Iran meeting odds collapsed from 73.8% to 54.1% in 24 hours on Polymarket, likely driven by rhetoric escalation and the Vance meeting odds cratering to 26.1%. Here's the thing: panic selling creates mispricings. Diplomatic backchannels stay warm even when public posturing heats up. The market is pricing in doomsday when history says meetings still happen. Counterintuitive play: the 54% floor looks too low for a 4-month window.

sidebar.headline: Vance's 26.1% is the real tell. Markets are overweighting his personal ideology into the odds when envoys and state departments still call the shots on back-channel diplomacy.
sidebar.label: Vance Meeting: 26.1%

sidebar.headline: Sports markets moving 15+ points intraday usually signal either sharp action or technical selloff, not fundamental news. Check if Bulls-Wizards is catching flow from unrelated political liquidations.
sidebar.label: Bulls vs. Wizards: 69.5%

sidebar.headline: Hormuz traffic at 26% feels like cascading fear. If sanctions talk is real, that's priced in. If it's theater, this bounces hard. Watch sanctions headline velocity over the next 48 hours.
sidebar.label: Hormuz Normal: 26.0%
